name: Baix Llobregat Agricultural Park Loop
name_original: El Prat – rodeo por el El Baix Llobregat
description: Cycle 26.6 miles (42.8 km) on this moderate racebike route around El Prat and El Baix Llobregat, exploring diverse landscapes.
region_intro: Road cycling around Cornellà De Llobregat offers varied terrain, from flat routes along the Llobregat River and Delta to more challenging climbs in the nearby Collserola Natural Park. The region provides access to coastal plains with views of the Mediterranean and extends towards the significant ascents of Montserrat Mountain. This diverse landscape caters to different cycling preferences and fitness levels. Best road cycling routes around Cornellà De Llobregat The most popular road cycling route is Baix Llobregat Agricultural Park Loop, a 26.6 miles (42.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 55 minutes to complete. This route offers flat sections through agricultural areas near the Llobregat River. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Gavà - Begues Climb – Church of Olesa de Bonesvalls loop from Cornellà, a moderate 39.0 miles (62.8 km) path. This route includes a notable climb, providing elevation changes and views. Local road cyclists also love the Garraf Coastal Road – Coastal Road to Castelldefels loop from Gavarra, a 37.8 miles (60.8 km) trail leading through coastal areas, often completed in about 2 hours 44 minutes. Road cycling around Cornellà De Llobregat is defined by river plains, coastal roads, and the hills of Collserola Natural Park. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Cornellà De Llobregat are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 22,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Cornellà De Llobregat's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling near Cornellà De Llobregat?
A: The region offers a diverse range of terrain for road cycling. You'll find flat routes along the Llobregat River and its delta, coastal plains leading to beaches like Castelldefels and Gavà, and more challenging climbs with significant elevation changes in the nearby Collserola Natural Park. For experienced cyclists seeking demanding ascents, routes extending towards Montserrat Mountain are also accessible.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Cornellà De Llobregat?
A: There are over 10 road cycling routes available around Cornellà De Llobregat, catering to various skill levels. This includes easy, moderate, and difficult options, ensuring there's a suitable ride for almost every cyclist.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Cornellà De Llobregat?
A: Yes, there are routes ideal for beginners. The area offers flat sections, particularly along the Llobregat River. An excellent option for an easier ride is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995626466/baix-llobregat-agricultural-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baix Llobregat Agricultural Park Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a mostly flat experience through agricultural areas.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a challenge, routes with significant elevation gain are available. Consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39557134" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carretera de Castellbisbal – Martorell's Devil's Bridge loop from Cornellà&lt;/a&gt;, which features substantial climbs. More demanding options also extend towards Montserrat Mountain, offering considerable ascents.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Cornellà De Llobregat?
A: The region generally experiences a mild and pleasant climate, making it suitable for cycling for much of the year. However, the most ideal times are during spring (April to June) and autumn (September to November), when temperatures are comfortable and the weather is typically stable.

Q: Are there scenic road cycling routes with notable landmarks or viewpoints?
A: Many routes offer scenic views and pass by interesting landmarks. Coastal routes provide Mediterranean vistas, while rides into Collserola Natural Park offer views of both the Barcelona and Vallés slopes. You can also find highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6393895" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Sant Ramon&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2687128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Pere Màrtir Summit and Observation Towers&lt;/a&gt; on routes that venture into the hills.

Q: Can I find routes that include waterfalls near Cornellà De Llobregat?
A: Yes, if you venture into the nearby Collserola Natural Park, you can find routes that pass by natural features like waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/936989" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Rierada Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a notable example within the park that can be incorporated into a cycling trip.

Q: Are there loop road cycling trails around Cornellà De Llobregat?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in the area are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1028232" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gavà - Begues Climb – Church of Olesa de Bonesvalls loop from Cornellà&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38153401" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garraf Coastal Road – Coastal Road to Castelldefels loop from Gavarra&lt;/a&gt;, offering varied scenery and challenges.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Cornellà De Llobregat?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 400 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, which allows for everything from flat river rides to challenging mountain ascents, and the accessibility to natural parks and coastal areas.

Q: Is it possible to access road cycling routes using public transport from Cornellà De Llobregat?
A: Cornellà De Llobregat is well-connected by public transport, making it convenient to reach starting points for various routes. Its strategic location allows for easy access to different cycling areas, including the Llobregat River, coastal plains, and the foothills of Collserola Natural Park.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling routes in the area?
A: While many road cycling routes are designed for more experienced riders, the flat sections along the Llobregat River and within the Llobregat Delta offer gentler terrain that can be suitable for families. These areas provide a safer and less strenuous environment for a leisurely ride.
tips:
Numerous industries were located in the Llobregat basin in the 19th and 20th centuries that took advantage of its energy and water, highlighting the so-called "industrial colonies". The river water receives numerous contributions of pollution from industries and liquid waste from the urban centers located along its course, although efforts are being made to reduce them. In addition, the Llobregat today suffers from a peculiar source of contamination due to residues from the potassium mines, which make its salinity high.

   The Llobregat basin is the source of drinking water for towns such as Sabadell, Hospitalet or much of Barcelona itself.

Perfect for riding a bike with almost no cars

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this ride?
faqs.answer: This racebike route, covering approximately 42.8 km (26.6 miles), usually takes around 1 hour and 56 minutes to complete. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your cycling speed and how often you stop.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is categorized as moderate and is generally considered great for any fitness level. It features mostly well-paved surfaces and a smooth, flat profile along the Llobregat River, making it accessible for less experienced cyclists. However, some segments might be unpaved, adding a slight challenge.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience a diverse mix of urban, rural, and natural landscapes. The route takes you through the agricultural areas of the Baix Llobregat region, offering views of fields and natural reserves. You'll also ride near the Delta del Llobregat nature reserve and enjoy sections along the Llobregat River.

faqs.question: What are the notable highlights or landmarks on this route?
faqs.answer: Key points of interest include the Llobregat River Crossing at Molins de Rei, the Sant Climent to Sant Boi Road, and the EuroVelo Bike Path Near Barcelona El Prat Airport. You'll also pass through the Parc Agrari del Baix Llobregat, which offers unique agricultural scenery.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this route?
faqs.answer: The Baix Llobregat region benefits from a privileged climate, making it suitable for cycling year-round. However, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for a comfortable ride, avoiding the peak heat of summer or cooler winter days.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route, especially within Parc Agrari del Baix Llobregat?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route or to pass through the Parc Agrari del Baix Llobregat. However, please respect the agricultural land and natural areas, staying on designated paths and trails.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through various landscapes, including urban and natural areas, it's primarily designed for road cycling. Bringing dogs, especially off-leash, might not be ideal due to traffic, agricultural zones, and potential interactions with wildlife in natural reserve areas. It's best to consider routes specifically designated as dog-friendly for your furry companions.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this cycling route?
faqs.answer: As the route starts and ends in El Prat de Llobregat, you can look for public parking options within the town. Many urban areas in the Baix Llobregat region offer street parking or dedicated parking lots. Consider starting near a public transport hub for easier access and parking.

faqs.question: How can I reach the starting point using public transport?
faqs.answer: El Prat de Llobregat is well-connected by public transport from Barcelona. You can take the metro (L9 Sud line) or various bus lines to reach the town center, which is a convenient starting point for this route.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that are part of the Bicivia 1, Camí Ral de Barcelona a Vilafranca, Bicivia 7, and the Ruta Mediterrània, among others. This offers opportunities to extend or modify your ride.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for food or drinks along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through El Prat de Llobregat and other towns in the Baix Llobregat region, you'll find various cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can stop for refreshments or a meal. There are also local businesses like Ciclistyk in El Prat de Llobregat that cater to cyclists.
